words.definitionEn: a statement saying that you strongly believe something to be true
words.exampleEn: He was correct in his assertion that the minister had been lying.

words.definitionEn: happening or existing in all parts of a particular country
words.exampleEn: a nationwide campaign

words.definitionEn: connected with the navy of a country
words.exampleEn: a naval base/officer/battle

words.definitionEn: the fact of not giving enough care or attention to something/somebody; the state of not receiving enough care or attention
words.exampleEn: The buildings are crumbling from years of neglect.

words.definitionEn: to fail to take care of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: She denies neglecting her baby.

words.definitionEn: located or living near or next to a place or person
words.exampleEn: a neighbouring house

words.definitionEn: a hollow place or structure that a bird makes or chooses for laying its eggs in and sheltering its young
words.exampleEn: sparrows building a nest of twigs and dry grass

words.definitionEn: a net amount of money is the amount that remains when nothing more is to be taken away
words.exampleEn: a net profit of £500

words.definitionEn: a report containing news of the activities of a club or organization that is sent regularly to all its members
words.exampleEn: Our sailing club produces a monthly newsletter.

words.definitionEn: a comfortable or suitable role, job, way of life, etc.
words.exampleEn: He eventually found his niche in sports journalism.

words.definitionEn: having or showing fine personal qualities that people admire, such as courage, honesty and care for others
words.exampleEn: a noble leader

words.definitionEn: if you nod, nod your head or your head nods, you move your head up and down to show agreement, understanding, etc.
words.exampleEn: I asked him if he would help me and he nodded.

words.definitionEn: to formally suggest that somebody/something should be chosen for an important role, prize, position, etc.
words.exampleEn: nominate somebody/something (for something), She has been nominated for the presidency.

words.definitionEn: the act of suggesting or choosing somebody as a candidate in an election, or for a job or an award; the fact of being suggested for this
words.exampleEn: Membership of the club is by nomination only.

words.definitionEn: a person who has been formally suggested for a job, a prize, etc.
words.exampleEn: He was chosen as the party's presidential nominee.

words.definitionEn: without the aim of making a profit
words.exampleEn: an independent non-profit organization

words.definitionEn: despite this fact
words.exampleEn: The book is too long but, nonetheless, informative and entertaining.

words.definitionEn: ideas, statements or beliefs that you think are silly or not true
words.exampleEn: Reports that he has resigned are nonsense.

words.definitionEn: 12 o’clock in the middle of the day
words.exampleEn: We should be there by noon.

words.definitionEn: deserving to be noticed or to receive attention; important
words.exampleEn: a notable success/achievement/example

words.definitionEn: used for giving a good or the most important example of something
words.exampleEn: The house had many drawbacks, most notably its price.

words.definitionEn: to formally or officially tell somebody about something
words.exampleEn: notify somebody, Competition winners will be notified by post.

words.definitionEn: well known for being bad
words.exampleEn: a notorious criminal

words.definitionEn: different from anything known before; new, interesting and often seeming slightly strange
words.exampleEn: a novel feature

words.definitionEn: a place where young children are cared for while their parents are at work; a day nursery or nursery school
words.exampleEn: at nursery, Her youngest child is at nursery now.

words.definitionEn: a reason why you do not like or are opposed to something; a statement about this
words.exampleEn: I'd like to come too, if you have no objection.

words.definitionEn: to force somebody to do something, by law, because it is a duty, etc.
words.exampleEn: Parents are obliged by law to send their children to school.

words.definitionEn: to completely fill your mind so that you cannot think of anything else, in a way that is not reasonable or normal
words.exampleEn: be obsessed by somebody/something, He's obsessed by computers.

words.definitionEn: the state in which a person’s mind is completely filled with thoughts of one particular thing or person in a way that is not reasonable or normal
words.exampleEn: Her fear of flying is bordering on obsession.

words.definitionEn: happening or done sometimes but not often
words.exampleEn: He works for us on an occasional basis.

words.definitionEn: something that happens or exists
words.exampleEn: a common/everyday/frequent/regular occurrence

words.definitionEn: the degree to which something is likely to happen
words.exampleEn: The odds are very much in our favour (= we are likely to succeed).

words.definitionEn: a statement that something will certainly be true or will certainly happen, particularly when there has been doubt about it
words.exampleEn: They called for assurances that the government is committed to its education policy.

words.definitionEn: something that is produced for other people to use, watch, enjoy, etc.
words.exampleEn: the latest offering from the Canadian-born writer

words.definitionEn: a child of a particular person or couple
words.exampleEn: the problems parents have with their teenage offspring

words.definitionEn: protection that a government gives to people who have left their own country, usually because they were in danger for political reasons
words.exampleEn: to seek/apply for/be granted asylum

words.definitionEn: connected with the way in which a business, machine, system, etc. works
words.exampleEn: operational activities/costs/difficulties

words.definitionEn: to choose to take or not to take a particular course of action
words.exampleEn: opt for/against something, After graduating she opted for a career in music.

words.definitionEn: connected with the sense of sight or the relationship between light and sight
words.exampleEn: optical effects

words.definitionEn: a feeling that good things will happen and that something will be successful
words.exampleEn: a mood of cautious optimism

words.definitionEn: spoken rather than written
words.exampleEn: a test of both oral and written French

words.definitionEn: connected with the way in which the different parts of something are arranged; connected with an organization
words.exampleEn: organizational changes within the party

words.definitionEn: a cruel and violent act, especially in a war
words.exampleEn: In the war, both sides committed atrocities.

words.definitionEn: a person’s basic beliefs or feelings about a particular subject
words.exampleEn: religious/political orientation

words.definitionEn: to happen or appear for the first time in a particular place or situation
words.exampleEn: The disease is thought to have originated in the tropics.

words.definitionEn: the sudden start of something unpleasant, especially violence or a disease
words.exampleEn: the outbreak of war

words.definitionEn: a trip that you go on for pleasure or education, usually with a group of people and lasting no more than one day
words.exampleEn: a family outing

words.definitionEn: a shop or an organization that sells goods made by a particular company or of a particular type
words.exampleEn: The business has 34 retail outlets in this state alone.

words.definitionEn: the attitude to life and the world of a particular person, group or culture
words.exampleEn: Travel broadens your outlook.

words.definitionEn: a strong feeling of shock and anger
words.exampleEn: The judge's remarks caused public outrage.

words.definitionEn: to make somebody very shocked and angry
words.exampleEn: He was outraged at the way he had been treated.

words.definitionEn: a person who is not accepted as a member of a society, group, etc.
words.exampleEn: Here she felt she would always be an outsider.

words.definitionEn: to fail to see or notice something
words.exampleEn: He seems to have overlooked one important fact.

words.definitionEn: too; very
words.exampleEn: I'm not overly fond of pasta.

words.definitionEn: to watch somebody/something and make sure that a job or an activity is done correctly
words.exampleEn: United Nations observers oversaw the elections.

words.definitionEn: to succeed in getting something, usually after a lot of effort
words.exampleEn: Most of our students attained five ‘A’ grades in their exams.

words.definitionEn: if something overturns, or if somebody overturns it, it turns into a position in which the top of it is where the bottom of it normally is or on its side
words.exampleEn: The car skidded and overturned.

words.definitionEn: to have such a strong emotional effect on somebody that it is difficult for them to resist or know how to react
words.exampleEn: She was overwhelmed by feelings of guilt.

words.definitionEn: very great or very strong; so powerful that you cannot resist it or decide how to react
words.exampleEn: The evidence against him was overwhelming.

words.definitionEn: a thick piece of soft material that is used, for example, for cleaning or protecting something or for holding liquid
words.exampleEn: medicated cleansing pads for sensitive skin

words.definitionEn: something that decides or limits the way in which something can be done
words.exampleEn: to set/define the parameters

words.definitionEn: the act of being present at a place, for example at school
words.exampleEn: Attendance at these lectures is not compulsory.

words.definitionEn: connected with a parent or parents
words.exampleEn: parental responsibility/rights

words.definitionEn: an area that has its own church and that a priest is responsible for
words.exampleEn: a parish church/priest

words.definitionEn: connected with a parliament; having a parliament
words.exampleEn: parliamentary elections

words.definitionEn: not complete or whole
words.exampleEn: It was only a partial solution to the problem.

words.definitionEn: partly; not completely
words.exampleEn: The road was partially blocked by a fallen tree.

words.definitionEn: the process of time going by
words.exampleEn: The colour of the wood darkens with the passing of time.

words.definitionEn: accepting what happens or what people do without trying to change anything or oppose them
words.exampleEn: He played a passive role in the relationship.

words.definitionEn: a minister in charge of a Christian church or group, especially in some Nonconformist churches
words.exampleEn: He was ordained a pastor in the Lutheran Church.

words.definitionEn: a small area of something, especially one which is different from the area around it
words.exampleEn: a black dog with a white patch on its back

words.definitionEn: an official right to be the only person to make, use or sell a product or an invention; a document that proves this
words.exampleEn: patent on something, to apply for/obtain/take out a patent on an invention

words.definitionEn: a track that serves as a path
words.exampleEn: They came out of the woods and onto a pathway.

words.definitionEn: a lawyer, especially one who can act for somebody in court
words.exampleEn: The prosecuting attorney began with a short opening statement.

words.definitionEn: the act of going to different parts of a building, an area, etc. to make sure that there is no trouble or crime
words.exampleEn: Security guards make regular patrols at night.

words.definitionEn: to go around an area or a building at regular times to check that it is safe and that there is no trouble
words.exampleEn: Troops patrolled the border day and night.

words.definitionEn: a person who gives money and support to artists and writers
words.exampleEn: Frederick the Great was the patron of many artists.

words.definitionEn: an impressive thing that is done or achieved after a lot of work
words.exampleEn: It was one of the President's greatest accomplishments.

words.definitionEn: the point when somebody/something is best, most successful, strongest, etc.
words.exampleEn: Traffic reaches its peak between 8 and 9 in the morning.

words.definitionEn: (especially in the past, or in poorer countries) a farmer who owns or rents a small piece of land
words.exampleEn: peasant farmers

words.definitionEn: strange or unusual, especially in a way that is unpleasant or makes you worried
words.exampleEn: a peculiar smell/taste

words.definitionEn: a quality or feature of somebody/something
words.exampleEn: Patience is one of the most important attributes in a teacher.

words.definitionEn: to continue to do something despite difficulties or opposition, in a way that can seem unreasonable
words.exampleEn: persist in doing something, Why do you persist in blaming yourself for what happened?

words.definitionEn: determined to do something despite difficulties, especially when other people are against you and think that you are being annoying or unreasonable
words.exampleEn: How do you deal with persistent salesmen who won't take no for an answer?

words.definitionEn: the people who work for an organization or one of the armed forces
words.exampleEn: There is a severe shortage of skilled personnel.

words.definitionEn: to say or believe that something is the result of a particular thing
words.exampleEn: She attributes her success to hard work and a little luck.

words.definitionEn: a written document signed by a large number of people that asks somebody in a position of authority to do or change something
words.exampleEn: petition against something, Would you like to sign our petition against experiments on animals?

words.definitionEn: a person who studies or writes about philosophy
words.exampleEn: We studied the writings of the Greek philosopher Aristotle.

words.definitionEn: connected with philosophy
words.exampleEn: the philosophical writings of Kant

words.definitionEn: a doctor, especially one who is a specialist in general medicine and not surgery
words.exampleEn: Dr Dennett is a practicing family physician in Atlanta.

words.definitionEn: a person who is the first to study and develop a particular area of knowledge, culture, etc. that other people then continue to develop
words.exampleEn: a pioneer in the field of microsurgery

words.definitionEn: when somebody pioneers something, they are one of the first people to do, discover or use something new
words.exampleEn: a new technique pioneered by surgeons in a London hospital

words.definitionEn: a series of pipes that are usually underground and are used for carrying oil, gas, etc. over long distances
words.exampleEn: There are plans to lay a gas pipeline through the region.

words.definitionEn: (especially in the past) a person on a ship who attacks other ships at sea in order to steal from them
words.exampleEn: There were reports that a pirate ship had come looking for treasure in the cove.

words.definitionEn: an official examination of business and financial records to see that they are true and correct
words.exampleEn: an annual audit

words.definitionEn: a large deep hole in the ground
words.exampleEn: We dug a deep pit in the yard.

words.definitionEn: according to a rule or the way that somebody says that something should be done
words.exampleEn: in accordance with legal requirements

words.definitionEn: a serious emotional request, especially for something needing action now
words.exampleEn: plea for something, She made an impassioned plea for help.

words.definitionEn: to ask somebody for something in a very strong and serious way
words.exampleEn: plead (with somebody) (to do something), She pleaded with him not to go.

words.definitionEn: a serious promise
words.exampleEn: pledge (of something), a pledge of support

words.definitionEn: to formally promise to give or do something
words.exampleEn: pledge something, Japan has pledged $100 million in humanitarian aid.

words.definitionEn: known to be real and what somebody claims it is and not a copy
words.exampleEn: I don't know if the painting is authentic.

words.definitionEn: a small plastic object with two or three metal pins that connects a piece of electrical equipment to the main supply of electricity
words.exampleEn: The iron is fitted with a three-pin plug.

words.definitionEn: to fill a hole with a substance or piece of material that fits tightly into it
words.exampleEn: He plugged the hole in the pipe with an old rag.

words.definitionEn: to move or make somebody/something move suddenly forwards and/or downwards
words.exampleEn: + adv./prep., She lost her balance and plunged 100 feet to her death.

words.definitionEn: a long thin straight piece of wood or metal, especially one with the end placed in the ground, used as a support
words.exampleEn: a tent pole

words.definitionEn: the process of questioning people who are representative of a larger group in order to get information about the general opinion
words.exampleEn: to carry out/conduct a poll

words.definitionEn: to give official permission for something, or for somebody to do something
words.exampleEn: authorize something, I can authorize payments up to £5 000.

words.definitionEn: a small area of still water, especially one that is artificial
words.exampleEn: a fish pond

words.definitionEn: to make a short explosive sound; to cause something to make this sound
words.exampleEn: the sound of corks popping

words.definitionEn: a car
words.exampleEn: the auto industry

words.definitionEn: a thin flat case used for carrying documents, drawings, etc.
words.exampleEn: I left my portfolio on the train.

words.definitionEn: to show somebody/something in a picture; to describe somebody/something in a piece of writing
words.exampleEn: The painting portrays the duke’s third wife.

words.definitionEn: existing, happening or made in the period after a war, especially the Second World War
words.exampleEn: the post-war years

words.definitionEn: to arrange for an event, etc. to take place at a later time or date than originally planned
words.exampleEn: postpone something, The game has already been postponed three times.

words.definitionEn: the freedom for a country, a region or an organization to govern itself independently
words.exampleEn: a campaign in Wales for greater autonomy

words.definitionEn: a person who works in a profession, especially medicine or law
words.exampleEn: dental practitioners

words.definitionEn: to give a religious talk in a public place, especially in a church during a service
words.exampleEn: She preached to the congregation about forgiveness.

words.definitionEn: an official action or decision that has happened in the past and that is seen as an example or a rule to be followed in a similar situation later
words.exampleEn: The ruling set a precedent for future libel cases.

words.definitionEn: the quality of being exact, accurate and careful
words.exampleEn: Her writing is imaginative but lacks precision.

words.definitionEn: an animal that kills and eats other animals
words.exampleEn: Some animals have no natural predators.

words.definitionEn: a person who did a job before somebody else
words.exampleEn: The new president reversed many of the policies of his predecessor.

words.definitionEn: to officially end a law, a system or an institution
words.exampleEn: This tax should be abolished.

words.definitionEn: the fact that something is possible to get, buy or find
words.exampleEn: the availability of cheap flights

words.definitionEn: mostly; mainly
words.exampleEn: She works in a predominantly male environment.

words.definitionEn: the state of being pregnant
words.exampleEn: a pregnancy test

words.definitionEn: an unreasonable dislike of or preference for a person, group, custom, etc., especially when it is based on their race, religion, sex, etc.
words.exampleEn: a victim of racial prejudice

words.definitionEn: happening before a more important action or event
words.exampleEn: After a few preliminary remarks he announced the winners.

words.definitionEn: most important, famous or successful
words.exampleEn: one of the country’s premier chefs

words.definitionEn: a statement or an idea that forms the basis for a reasonable line of argument
words.exampleEn: the basic premise of her argument

words.definitionEn: an amount of money that you pay once or regularly for an insurance policy
words.exampleEn: a monthly premium of £6.25

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody to take a particular medicine or have a particular treatment; to write a prescription for a particular medicine, etc.
words.exampleEn: prescribe something, Valium is usually prescribed to treat anxiety.

words.definitionEn: an official piece of paper on which a doctor writes the type of medicine you should have, and which enables you to get it from a chemist's
words.exampleEn: prescription for something, The doctor gave me a prescription for antibiotics.

words.definitionEn: at the time you are speaking or writing; now
words.exampleEn: The crime is presently being investigated by the police.

words.definitionEn: the act of keeping something in its original state or in good condition
words.exampleEn: building/environmental/food preservation
